message:
perf(btree): share default CollationRegistry across cursors (OPT-1)

Profile of INSERT hot path (perf-update-delete 500K rows, 10.7K samples
at 1999Hz) showed `HashMap<String, Arc<dyn CollationFunction>>::insert`
at 0.9% self-time and `Arc<Mutex<CollationRegistry>>::drop_slow` at
0.39% — a new `CollationRegistry` was being constructed (with 3
HashMap inserts for BINARY/NOCASE/RTRIM) inside `BtCursor::new()`, which
fires for every direct-simple INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

The default registry's content never changes (the 3 built-ins are
always the same), and the overwhelming majority of cursor constructions
— every table cursor, every direct-simple DML path — never mutates
the registry at all. Custom-collation callers still override via
`set_index_collation_context`.

Fix: share one `Arc<Mutex<CollationRegistry>>` via a `OnceLock`. The
first `BtCursor::new()` constructs it; every subsequent construction
is an `Arc::clone` (one atomic refcount bump) instead of a full
registry + Arc + Mutex + HashMap allocation.

Expected savings: ~1.3% self-time on INSERT hot path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
